metric_value: $16 billion multi-national corporation
notes: Kodak corporate scale cited to establish credibility for software acquisition

metric_value: Kodak paid $260 million in cash for Wang's software business
notes: Deal included Wang's workflow imaging COLD DM and HSM technology plus Microsoft relationship continuation

metric_value: 700 employees worldwide post-acquisition
notes: Robert Weiler continues as president of Eastman Software subsidiary

metric_value: 20000 customers in Kodak BIS installed base
notes: Large installed base cited as primary target for Eastman Software technology deployment

metric_value: $1.4 billion annual revenue in fiscal year 1997
notes: Wang services business retained by Wang Laboratories after software division sale

metric_value: More than 100% revenue growth for last two years in Wang Software business unit
notes: Strong growth trajectory cited as validation of software asset value

metric_value: Aberdeen believes Eastman Software is extremely well-positioned as independent Kodak subsidiary to expand leadership in document-intensive businesses
notes: Strong Aberdeen endorsement of acquisition rationale and strategic positioning

metric_value: Kodak sold troubled Eastman Software unit in August 2000 to private investment group; financial terms undisclosed but widely reported as significant loss on $260M investment
notes: Source: Rochester Business Journal Aug 23 2000 — Eastman Software had been on short list of divestitures for more than a year

metric_value: Eastman Software's workflow capabilities will position it as dominant force in desktop and departmental server market
notes: Aberdeen forecasts dominant position for Eastman Software in workflow/desktop market

metric_value: Eastman Software was sold before achieving dominant position; workflow market moved to SharePoint Lotus Notes and later BPM platforms; Eastman Software technology did not achieve predicted dominance
notes: Microsoft SharePoint (2001) became dominant workflow/document platform; Eastman Software was not a significant player

metric_value: Wang imaging technology embedded in Windows 95 and NT 4.0; HSM joint development continued with Eastman Software
notes: Microsoft distribution channel viewed as major strategic asset inherited by Eastman Software

metric_value: Windows XP/2000 did not further develop Wang/Eastman imaging integration; Microsoft shifted focus to SharePoint for document management rather than continuing Wang-era imaging integration
notes: Microsoft's own document management platform (SharePoint 2001) superseded the Wang-era imaging integration strategy

metric_value: COLD dramatically improves distribution options for service bureaus and supplements customers' existing COM capabilities
notes: Aberdeen views COLD as important new capability Kodak customers gain from Eastman Software

metric_value: COLD technology became obsolete by mid-2000s as internet-based document delivery and digital archiving replaced optical disk output; market largely gone by 2005
notes: Internet delivery and PDF/digital formats made COLD largely redundant

metric_value: Aberdeen forecasts HSM technology will play key role as organizations need access to digital images in multi-terabyte decision support systems
notes: Aberdeen predicts Eastman Software HSM critical for data warehouse environments

metric_value: HSM evolved into Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) and later cloud storage tiering; Eastman Software HSM did not achieve predicted role; Veritas StorageExec and IBM Tivoli became market leaders
notes: HSM as category survived but Eastman Software did not capture this market

metric_value: Kodak has nine customer-focused business units; customer-centric rather than product-oriented view of market
notes: Kodak's structure presented as strength enabling targeted customer service

metric_value: Kodak filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in January 2012; digital photography disrupted core film business; emerged in 2013 as much smaller commercial printing company
notes: Kodak's customer-centric structure did not protect it from technological disruption of its core film business

metric_value: IDAR provides backbone of complete enterprise document life cycle solution combining imaging workflow COLD DM and HSM
notes: Aberdeen presents IDAR as comprehensive platform strategy

metric_value: Document imaging and work management entering new stage of explosive growth; production-level capabilities now deliverable at lower price points to distributed departmental environments
notes: Aberdeen correctly identifies democratization trend in document imaging

metric_value: Wang acquired Sigma Imaging Avail Systems and Vantage Technologies to enhance market position and accelerate NT platform transition
notes: Wang's roll-up strategy of imaging/DM vendors presented as validation of portfolio value

metric_value: Aberdeen field interviews indicate considerable enthusiasm from Wang Software customers resellers and partners about Kodak's document-imaging focus and additional R&D funding
notes: Customer/partner optimism cited but may reflect confirmation bias in Aberdeen's bullish view
